plan.reasoning: No prior attempts exist, so I should propose the best physically motivated initial adsorption plan rather than terminate. SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=[NH], i.e. NNH/diazene radical-like form as written. The provided atom list contains two N atoms: index 0 is SP2 with 1 radical electron, and index 1 is SP2 with 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch between the requested species NNH and the SMILES. Because I plan to use the terminal radical N atom for binding, ReactiveSpecies is appropriate and chemically valid since atom 0 explicitly has an unpaired electron. For surface chemistry on Cu/Ag(111), N-containing radicals generally bind more strongly to Cu than Ag, and an initial low-energy guess should prioritize Cu-rich sites. Since only one adsorbate atom has a radical electron, a monodentate starting mode is most defensible. An ontop Cu site is the cleanest first trial and often a good entry point for N-centered radicals; it avoids forcing an unsupported bidentate geometry at the outset. Available sites include ontop Cu, so this is allowed. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on the alloy surface; therefore the first systematic trial should target the most likely strongest binding atom-surface pair: terminal radical N (index 0) on Cu ontop.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=[NH], i.e. NNH/diazene radical-like species with two heavy atoms. The provided atom list is consistent and must be used exactly: atom 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; atom 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. Because atom 0 has an unpaired electron, a ReactiveSpecies treatment is chemically justified if binding through atom 0. History analysis: two plans have already been explored. (1) ontop on Cu using a Cu site led to dissociation, so that is a failure and should not be repeated. (2) a bridge Cu-Cu attempt actually relaxed into a hollow Cu-Cu-Cu configuration and is the current best successful intact adsorption at -2.165 eV. Since the ontop Cu attempt was even lower in energy but dissociated, stronger binding motifs on Cu-rich environments may favor decomposition. Therefore, to search for a lower-energy intact adsorption, it is sensible to test a chemically distinct alloy environment rather than another pure Cu-rich site. Available untested high-symmetry environments include ontop Ag, bridge Ag-Cu, hollow Ag-Cu-Cu, and hollow Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu. The user wants the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on this alloy surface, so the next systematic step is to probe an alloy bridge environment that may stabilize intact NNH through the radical terminal N while reducing overbinding relative to pure Cu. Binding should use atom 0 only, since that is the radical center and the most chemically appropriate anchor for ReactiveSpecies. A bridge Ag-Cu site is completely new relative to the history and respects the available site list.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=[NH], i.e. NNH/diazenyl-like species. The provided atom list has two N atoms only, indices 0 and 1, both SP2. Atom 0 has 1 radical electron, atom 1 has 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch between the user request for NNH and the SMILES. Because atom 0 is radical-bearing, ReactiveSpecies treatment is chemically appropriate if binding through atom 0. History analysis: three plans have already been attempted. Ontop on Cu using one Cu atom failed by dissociation, so that pathway is not acceptable even though the energy is low. Bridge on Cu-Cu succeeded but actually relaxed into a hollow Cu-Cu-Cu geometry and is currently the best successful intact adsorption at -2.165 eV. Bridge on Ag-Cu also succeeded but relaxed into a hollow Ag-Cu-Cu geometry with weaker adsorption at -1.801 eV. No ontop Ag, no hollow Cu-Cu-Cu direct start, and no hollow Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu direct start have been tested yet. Since the successful bridge starts both relaxed into hollow sites, the surface clearly favors hollow coordination for this adsorbate. To systematically search for a lower-energy intact state, the most logical unexplored high-symmetry candidate is a direct hollow trial in the all-Cu local environment, because the Cu-rich environment already outperformed the Ag-containing one and Cu ontop caused strong reactivity. User intent is to find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on this alloy surface. Adsorbate analysis: NNH has two N atoms; atom 0 is the radical terminal N and is the best chemically justified anchoring atom for ReactiveSpecies adsorption. Surface analysis: available hollow motifs include Ag-Cu-Cu, Cu-Cu-Cu, and Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu. Since Cu-Cu-Cu gave the best intact result after relaxation from bridge, directly targeting hollow on Cu-Cu-Cu is the best new plan and is distinct from all previous plans. This is not a duplicate-convergence tag case, so termination is not warranted yet.

plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=[NH], i.e. NNH. The provided heavy-atom list has two nitrogens only: index 0 is N with SP2 hybridization and 1 radical electron; index 1 is N with SP2 hybridization and 0 radical electrons. There is no mismatch between the user request and the SMILES. Because one binding candidate (index 0) is explicitly radical, treating this as ReactiveSpecies is chemically consistent if we bind through that atom. History analysis: tested plans include ontop on Cu via a single Cu atom, which led to dissociation and is therefore a failure despite the low energy; bridge on Cu-Cu succeeded but relaxed into an FCC hollow Cu-Cu-Cu site and is currently the best non-dissociative state at -2.165 eV; bridge on Ag-Cu succeeded and relaxed into an HCP-like Ag-Cu-Cu hollow at -1.801 eV; hollow on Cu-Cu-Cu succeeded but remained less stable at -1.741 eV. Therefore, Cu-rich environments are favored over Ag-containing ones, and the strongest intact adsorption found so far is associated with Cu-only coordination. Remaining untested major high-symmetry site families from the allowed list are ontop on Ag and hollow on Ag-Cu-Cu and Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu. Since Ag-containing sites were already weaker than Cu-only sites, and Cu-rich hollow/bridge attempts have already either found the best intact state or slipped among related hollows, the only still-plausible unexplored high-symmetry option is the mixed Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu hollow-4 environment, which could offer a distinct alloy fingerprint not yet sampled. I should not repeat any previous exact plan, so I will propose a new hollow plan on Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu. User core intent is simply to locate the lowest-energy intact adsorption configuration for NNH on this alloy surface. Adsorbate analysis: NNH can plausibly bind through the terminal radical N atom (index 0); using index 1 as ReactiveSpecies would violate the radical rule. Surface analysis: Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu is explicitly listed as an available hollow site combination, so it is a valid new test. This is a systematic final exploration of the remaining distinct hollow alloy environment rather than inventing an unreasonable orientation.
